briefing_memo: WE Charity, based in Ontario, has received $5,197,155 in federal funding to date. The federal ethics commissioner found that former Finance Minister Morneau breached the Conflict of Interest Act regarding a WE Charity contract, though Prime Minister Trudeau was cleared of wrongdoing (CBC, July 2020). The organization shows two T3010 filing violations and a maximum overhead ratio of 15.2%, though no court cases or sanctions are recorded. Given the past ethics breach finding and moderate filing irregularities, an elevated due-diligence review is recommended before any next disbursement, though the charity retains its CRA designation and no current regulatory actions are pending.

actions.title: Conduct compliance review of T3010 filing practices
actions.rationale: Two T3010 violations detected indicate potential irregularities in charitable reporting obligations that require formal assessment

actions.title: Request documentation for overhead expenditure justification
actions.rationale: Overhead ratio of 15.2% approaches threshold levels and warrants verification against program delivery outcomes given federal funding of $5.2M

actions.title: Track ongoing media coverage and regulatory developments
actions.rationale: One medium-severity news item indicates public attention that may evolve; continued monitoring ensures awareness of emerging issues
